identity.short_name: Counting units with forced local order $p$ and global order $p^2$ via CRT, lifting, and exact-order counting

problem_spec.summary:
Let $p,q,r$ be distinct odd primes with $a\ge 2$, $b\ge 1$, $c\ge 1$ and assume $p\mid(q-1)$ and $p^2\mid(r-1)$.
Set $M=p^a q^b r^c$ and count units $x\pmod M$ satisfying a mixed local-global system:
$v_p(x-1)=a-1$ forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p^a}(x)=p$ by a binomial/LTE valuation jump,
$x^p\equiv 1\pmod{q^b}$ with $v_q(x-1)=0$ selects exactly $p-1$ nontrivial $p$th roots by Hensel lifting,
and the global constraint $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$ forces $\operatorname{ord}_{r^c}(x)=p^2$ via the lcm-of-orders rule under CRT.
The $r$-component count is then $\varphi(p^2)$ in a cyclic unit group.
givens: Define $S$ to be the set of units $x\pmod M$ such that $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$, $v_p(x-1)=a-1$, $x^p\equiv 1\pmod{q^b}$, and $v_q(x-1)=0$.
ask: Let $C=|S|$. Output the unique integer $A\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$ with $A\equiv C\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: Sets the target global order $p^2$ and the forced local order $p$ modulo $p^a$.

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: Ensures existence of nontrivial $p$th roots of unity modulo $q^b$.

parameter(s).name: r
parameter(s).purpose: Ensures existence of elements of exact order $p^2$ in $(\mathbb Z/r^c\mathbb Z)^\times$.

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: Depth for the exact valuation constraint $v_p(x-1)=a-1$.

invariant(s).name: crt_factorization
invariant(s).statement:
With $M=p^a q^b r^c$ and $\gcd(p^a,q^b)=\gcd(p^a,r^c)=\gcd(q^b,r^c)=1$, CRT gives a bijection
$x\pmod M \leftrightarrow (x\pmod{p^a},x\pmod{q^b},x\pmod{r^c})$ for residues, and an isomorphism on unit groups.

invariant(s).name: order_lcm_under_crt
invariant(s).statement:
For a unit $x\pmod M$ with components $(x_p,x_q,x_r)$ under CRT,
$\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=\operatorname{lcm}(\operatorname{ord}_{p^a}(x_p),\operatorname{ord}_{q^b}(x_q),\operatorname{ord}_{r^c}(x_r))$.

invariant(s).name: valuation_forces_local_order_p
invariant(s).statement: If $v_p(x-1)=a-1\ge 1$, then a binomial/LTE valuation jump gives $x^p\equiv 1\pmod{p^a}$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{p^a}(x)=p$.

invariant(s).name: hensel_filters_nontrivial_p_roots_mod_qb
invariant(s).statement:
Under $p\mid(q-1)$, the congruence $x^p\equiv 1\pmod{q^b}$ has exactly $p$ solutions, each lifting uniquely from modulo $q$,
and exactly one satisfies $x\equiv 1\pmod q$, so imposing $v_q(x-1)=0$ leaves exactly $p-1$ solutions of order $p$.

invariant(s).name: global_order_forces_r_component
invariant(s).statement:
If $\operatorname{ord}_{p^a}(x_p)=p$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{q^b}(x_q)=p$, then the global constraint $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$
forces $\operatorname{ord}_{r^c}(x_r)=p^2$ because $\operatorname{lcm}(p,p,m)=p^2$ implies $m$ is divisible by $p^2$.

essential_assumption(s): The primes are odd and pairwise distinct so CRT applies cleanly and lifting steps avoid $p=2$ pathologies.
The guards $p\mid(q-1)$ and $p^2\mid(r-1)$ ensure the local solution sets at $q^b$ and $r^c$ are nonempty.
$a\ge 2$ ensures $v_p(x-1)=a-1\ge 1$ so the valuation jump at $p$ is nontrivial.
uniqueness_certificate(s):
CRT splits residues into independent components. The $p$-adic valuation constraint forces exactly $p-1$ classes modulo $p^a$ and forces local order $p$.
The $q$-adic constraints select exactly $p-1$ nontrivial $p$th roots modulo $q^b$ by cyclic root counting and Hensel lifting.
With these local orders fixed as $p$ and $p$, the global constraint $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$ forces the $r$-component to have exact order $p^2$.
The unit group modulo $r^c$ is cyclic for odd $r$, so there are exactly $\varphi(p^2)$ such elements.
Therefore $C=(p-1)(p-1)\varphi(p^2)$ is forced and the output modulo $1000$ is unique.

technique(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem
inputs: Factorization $M=p^a q^b r^c$ with pairwise coprime factors
technique(s).purpose: Reduce global congruence and order conditions to local conditions and combine counts.
outputs: A bijection between $S$ and a set of compatible CRT triples

technique(s).name: p_adic_valuation_filtering
technique(s).purpose: Parametrize admissible residue classes by exact divisibility.
outputs: Finite explicit local parametrizations such as $x\equiv 1+p^{a-1}t$

technique(s).name: lifting_the_exponent
inputs: Binomial valuation jump in $(1+p^{a-1}t)^p-1$ for odd $p$
technique(s).purpose: Force $x^p\equiv 1\pmod{p^a}$ from the exact valuation at $p$.

technique(s).name: cyclic_group_root_count
inputs: Cyclicity of $(\mathbb Z/\ell\mathbb Z)^\times$ for prime $\ell$
technique(s).purpose: Count solutions to $y^k=1$ in prime-modulus unit groups.
outputs: Root counts such as exactly $\gcd(k,\ell-1)$ solutions modulo $\ell$

technique(s).name: hensel_lifting
inputs: Simple-root lifting for $f(X)=X^p-1$ from modulo $q$ to modulo $q^b$
technique(s).purpose: Count and classify $p$th roots modulo $q^b$ and separate the unique root congruent to $1$ modulo $q$.

technique(s).name: order_lcm_in_direct_product
technique(s).purpose: Translate $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$ into forced local order at the $r$-component.

theorem(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: Bijection of residues and unit-group decomposition modulo coprime factors.

theorem(s).name: lifting_the_exponent_lemma
theorem(s).role: Computes the $p$-adic valuation of $(1+p^{a-1}t)^p-1$ for odd $p$.

theorem(s).name: hensel_lemma_simple_root
theorem(s).role: Unique lifting of simple roots from modulo $q$ to modulo $q^2$ and hence to modulo $q^b$.

theorem(s).name: cyclicity_of_prime_modulus_unit_group
theorem(s).role: For prime $\ell$, the group $(\mathbb Z/\ell\mathbb Z)^\times$ is cyclic of order $\ell-1$.

theorem(s).name: cyclic_group_exact_order_phi
theorem(s).role: In a cyclic group, the number of elements of exact order $d$ is $\varphi(d)$.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Local-to-global structure: CRT converts a global constraint into local constraints plus an $\operatorname{lcm}$ coupling.

critical_trick(s): Use an exact $p$-adic valuation to force local order $p$ by a binomial/LTE valuation jump.
Use cyclic root counting modulo a prime and Hensel lifting to lift and filter $p$th roots modulo $q^b$.
Use the order $\operatorname{lcm}$ rule under CRT to force the remaining component to carry the extra factor of $p$.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: GCD checks, inverses for CRT, and small helper functions.

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: Cartesian products to enumerate CRT triples.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Multiplicative order computation and CRT helper routines for verification.

task(s).name: crt_enumeration_verification
task(s).reason: Enumerate the local solution sets modulo $5^4$, $11^2$, and $101$, combine them by CRT, and directly verify all defining congruences and $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=25$ for each combined solution.
